mapping.a_element: integral equation formulation of the magnetic induction equation
mapping.b_element: kinematic α² dynamo model with anisotropic α tensor

mapping.a_element: electrically conducting fluid region and its boundary as computational domain
mapping.b_element: finite cylinder geometry with specified aspect ratio and annular flow space
columnar flow derived anisotropic α effect driving dynamo action
critical aspect ratio for dominant magnetic field structure transition

mapping.a_element: externally added layers modifying dynamo onset
mapping.b_element: thickness of annular space affecting dynamo field structure

mapping.a_element: simulation of VKS and Riga dynamo experiments

brief_justification: Both papers investigate kinematic dynamo mechanisms in cylindrical geometries, where the core machinery involves solving the magnetic induction equation—either via integral equation transformation (A) or via an anisotropic α² model (B). The role of geometry (aspect ratio, annular space) and flow-driven dynamo action (Beltrami-like flows ↔ columnar flow-derived α effect) maps directly, with thresholds (self-excitation ↔ critical aspect ratio) governing field structure transitions.
